verses.text: A lamentation for Pharaoh, likened to a young lion and a whale that troubles the waters. Restless power. He stirred and muddied the nations to his own glory.
A lamentation for Pharaoh, likened to a young lion and a whale that troubles the waters. Restless power. He stirred and muddied the nations to his own glory.
God will spread His net over him, drag him to land, and fill the valleys with his fallen. The monster caught. The mightiest is helpless once God lays His snare.
God will spread His net over him, drag him to land, and fill the valleys with his fallen. The monster caught. The mightiest is helpless once God lays His snare.
God will spread His net over him, drag him to land, and fill the valleys with his fallen. The monster caught. The mightiest is helpless once God lays His snare.
His blood shall water the land, and God will darken the heavens and stars over him. Cosmic mourning. The fall of the great is pictured as a darkening of the very sky.
His blood shall water the land, and God will darken the heavens and stars over him. Cosmic mourning. The fall of the great is pictured as a darkening of the very sky.
His blood shall water the land, and God will darken the heavens and stars over him. Cosmic mourning. The fall of the great is pictured as a darkening of the very sky.
Many peoples shall be troubled, and kings tremble for fear at his fall. Dread spreads. The ruin of one tyrant unsettles all who trusted in such power.
Many peoples shall be troubled, and kings tremble for fear at his fall. Dread spreads. The ruin of one tyrant unsettles all who trusted in such power.
The sword of Babylon shall come, and the mighty shall cut down his multitude. The instrument named. God appoints the very hand that will humble Egypt.
The sword of Babylon shall come, and the mighty shall cut down his multitude. The instrument named. God appoints the very hand that will humble Egypt.
He will destroy the beasts by the waters and make the rivers clear; when Egypt is desolate, they shall know the LORD. Stillness after tumult. Judgment teaches the lesson pride refused.
He will destroy the beasts by the waters and make the rivers clear; when Egypt is desolate, they shall know the LORD. Stillness after tumult. Judgment teaches the lesson pride refused.
He will destroy the beasts by the waters and make the rivers clear; when Egypt is desolate, they shall know the LORD. Stillness after tumult. Judgment teaches the lesson pride refused.
This is the dirge the daughters of the nations shall sing for Egypt. A song of sorrow. The proud are remembered at last only in lament.
The mighty in the grave speak of him; he goes down to lie with the uncircumcised, slain by the sword. Fellowship of the fallen. Death levels the great with the despised.
The mighty in the grave speak of him; he goes down to lie with the uncircumcised, slain by the sword. Fellowship of the fallen. Death levels the great with the despised.
The mighty in the grave speak of him; he goes down to lie with the uncircumcised, slain by the sword. Fellowship of the fallen. Death levels the great with the despised.
The mighty in the grave speak of him; he goes down to lie with the uncircumcised, slain by the sword. Fellowship of the fallen. Death levels the great with the despised.
The mighty in the grave speak of him; he goes down to lie with the uncircumcised, slain by the sword. Fellowship of the fallen. Death levels the great with the despised.
Asshur is there with her slain, and Elam likewise, bearing their shame in the pit. Former powers laid low. The empires that terrified the earth lie silent together.
Asshur is there with her slain, and Elam likewise, bearing their shame in the pit. Former powers laid low. The empires that terrified the earth lie silent together.
Asshur is there with her slain, and Elam likewise, bearing their shame in the pit. Former powers laid low. The empires that terrified the earth lie silent together.
Asshur is there with her slain, and Elam likewise, bearing their shame in the pit. Former powers laid low. The empires that terrified the earth lie silent together.
Meshech and Tubal and their hordes are there; Pharaoh shall be broken and lie among them. No escape. He joins the long company of the proud brought down.
Meshech and Tubal and their hordes are there; Pharaoh shall be broken and lie among them. No escape. He joins the long company of the proud brought down.
Meshech and Tubal and their hordes are there; Pharaoh shall be broken and lie among them. No escape. He joins the long company of the proud brought down.
Edom, the northern princes, and the Zidonians are there too, gone down with the slain. All gathered. The grave receives kings and nations without distinction.
Edom, the northern princes, and the Zidonians are there too, gone down with the slain. All gathered. The grave receives kings and nations without distinction.
Pharaoh shall see them and be 'comforted' over his multitude, for God spread His terror in the land of the living. Cold comfort. To share the doom of the proud is no true consolation.
